title: Choose the Correct Map Object Selection Mode Before Inspecting a Map Item
summary: Use the Map Monitor left-side object selection controls to choose the intended inspection mode before clicking a clickable map item. Supported selection modes shown in the source are robot, container, cell, and charger.
when_to_use: Use when reviewing clickable items on the Map Monitor and you need the system to interpret the next map click as a specific object type for inspection, such as a robot, container, cell, or charger.
not_for: Do not use this runbook as guidance for the downstream inspection details shown after the map item is clicked; the source does not describe those results.
Do not use this runbook for recovery, control, or movement actions; it only covers selecting the correct map object inspection mode before clicking.

responsibilities: Choose the intended object selection mode before clicking the map
Click the corresponding map item for inspection

safety_notes: This source presents the task as an inspection aid and does not describe any physical intervention.
If the clicked map item does not inspect as intended, verify the correct selection mode was chosen first.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the Map Monitor screen

access_required: Access to the Map Monitor screen
system_preconditions: The user is viewing the Map Monitor screen
The left-side object selection controls are available
A clickable map item is present for inspection

visual_references.description: Map Monitor Quick Select frame showing the left-side panel and object selection controls for robot, container, cell, and charger.

steps.title: Open Map Monitor and locate the object selection panel
steps.purpose: Identify the controls that determine how the next map click will be interpreted.
steps.instruction: Open the Map Monitor screen and locate the left-side panel with the object selection icons.
steps.expected_result: The left-side selection controls are visible on the Map Monitor screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The panel shows selectable object-type controls including robot, container, cell, and charger.
steps.failure_condition: The user cannot find the left-side object selection controls on the Map Monitor screen.

actions.label: Locate object selection controls
actions.exact_action: Open the Map Monitor screen and locate the left-side panel with object selection icons.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Left-side panel showing Robot, Container, Cell, and Charger selection controls.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the Map Monitor screen is not available.
Escalate if the left-side object selection controls are missing or cannot be identified.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Certain map items are clickable, but users must first choose the object type to inspect from the left-side panel: robot, container, cell, or charger.

steps.title: Decide which object type you need to inspect
steps.purpose: Ensure the next map click is interpreted as the intended object type.
steps.instruction: Before clicking an item on the map, decide which object type you want to inspect.
steps.expected_result: The intended inspection target is identified as one of the supported object types.
steps.healthy_condition: The user knows whether the next map click should inspect a robot, container, cell, or charger.
steps.failure_condition: The user clicks the map without first determining the intended object type.

actions.label: Choose intended object type
actions.exact_action: Before clicking an item on the map, decide which object type you want to inspect.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The object-type choices available on the left panel: robot, container, cell, and charger.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if it is unclear which object type should be inspected.
Escalate if the available selection modes do not match the object the user needs to inspect.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Users must first choose the object type to inspect from the left-side panel: robot, container, cell, or charger.

steps.title: Select the matching object type icon
steps.purpose: Set the map inspection mode to the correct object category before clicking the map.
steps.instruction: Click the matching selection icon: robot, container, cell, or charger.
steps.expected_result: The correct object selection mode is set for the next map click.
steps.healthy_condition: The selected mode matches the object type the user intends to inspect.
steps.failure_condition: A different selection mode is chosen, causing the next map click to be interpreted incorrectly.

actions.label: Select object type mode
actions.exact_action: Click the matching selection icon: robot, container, cell, or charger.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The left-side object selection icons used to choose robot, container, cell, or charger mode.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the correct icon cannot be selected.
Escalate if the interface does not allow choosing robot, container, cell, or charger selection mode.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Certain map items are clickable, but users must first choose the object type to inspect from the left-side panel: robot, container, cell, or charger.

steps.title: Click the corresponding map item to inspect it
steps.purpose: Inspect the intended map object using the selected object-type mode.
steps.instruction: After selecting the object type, click the corresponding item on the map to inspect it.
steps.expected_result: The map click is interpreted using the selected object type, allowing inspection of the intended item.
steps.healthy_condition: The clicked item is inspected as the intended object type.
steps.failure_condition: Clicking the map item does not inspect the intended object, indicating the wrong selection mode may have been chosen first.

actions.label: Click corresponding map item
actions.exact_action: After selecting the object type, click the corresponding item on the map to inspect it.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The map area used after selecting an object type, where the corresponding item is clicked for inspection.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if clicking the map item does not inspect the intended object.
Verify the correct selection mode was chosen first if the wrong object behavior occurs.
Escalate if repeated attempts with the correct selection mode still do not allow inspection.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Certain map items are clickable, but users must first choose the object type to inspect from the left-side panel: robot, container, cell, or charger.

success_criteria: The correct object selection mode is chosen before clicking the map.
The intended map item is inspected as the selected object type.
The user can use the left-side controls to choose among robot, container, cell, or charger before inspection.
healthy_conditions: The Map Monitor left-side object selection controls are visible.
The selected mode matches the intended inspection target.
Clicking the map after selecting the mode inspects the intended object type.
failure_conditions: The left-side object selection controls cannot be located.
The wrong object type is selected before clicking the map.
Clicking a map item does not inspect the intended object.
The source does not describe the resulting inspection details after the map item is clicked.
escalation_guidance: If clicking a map item does not inspect the intended object, verify that the correct selection mode was chosen first.
Escalate if the correct selection mode is chosen but the map still does not allow inspection of the intended object.
Escalate when additional detail is needed about the inspection results after clicking, because the source does not provide that information.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Map Monitor Quick Select area and left-side object selection controls for robot, container, cell, and charger.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Certain map items are clickable, but users must first choose the object type to inspect from the left-side panel: robot, container, cell, or charger.

missing_details: The source does not describe the detailed inspection output shown after clicking the selected map item.
The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not specify whether production stop or LOTO is required; none is stated.
The source does not define supporting roles beyond the primary L1 support framing.
